title: RAG vs. Fine-Tuning: The Decision
concept: Use RAG when you need to access external, frequently changing databases; use fine-tuning to train specific formatting or style habits.

exercises.prompt: What is the main difference between RAG and Fine-Tuning?

options.text: They are exactly the same thing.
RAG fetches external info; Fine-Tuning trains internal behavior.
RAG is only used for image generation.

exercises.explain: RAG is designed to fetch fresh, external data before generating an answer, while Fine-Tuning modifies the model's internal habits and style.

cards.title: Which Path to Choose?
cards.body: Should you teach your AI to search the web, or train it to write in a special style? Choosing the right tool saves you time, money, and prevents embarrassing AI mistakes!

cards.title: Making the Choice
cards.body: To choose between these tools, look at your goal. Use RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) if your AI needs to search external, constantly changing databases for fresh facts. Use Fine-Tuning if you want the AI to learn a specific writing style, formatting rule, or tone.

cards.title: The Open-Book Exam
cards.body: Imagine you have an open-book exam. RAG is like giving you a textbook that updates in real time—you can look up facts on the fly. Fine-Tuning is like practicing practice questions for weeks so you master the tone and format of the test.

cards.title: Real-World Decisions
cards.body: A medical assistant AI needs to know the latest clinical trials updated today: use RAG to search the database. An AI needs to write legal summaries using strict courtroom formatting rules: use Fine-Tuning to teach it the format.

cards.title: The Fact Myth
cards.body: Many believe fine-tuning is the only way to teach an AI new facts. In reality, fine-tuning is terrible for facts because they change! RAG is much cheaper and easier for keeping information up to date.

cards.title: Comparison Guide
cards.body: Use this quick guide to decide which technique fits your project's main goal.

left.text: Best for external, frequently updating facts and database lookups.

right.text: Best for teaching specific style, tone, format, and custom behavior.

exercises.prompt: If you want an AI to write code matching your company's strict formatting guidelines, which method should you choose?
exercises.explain: Fine-tuning changes how the model behaves and formats its output, making it perfect for learning a specific brand voice or code style.

exercises.prompt: True or False: RAG is better than fine-tuning when your data changes hourly.
exercises.explain: RAG connects the AI to a live source of truth, making it the best choice for dynamic, frequently changing information.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Choose the right tool for live database access.
exercises.sentence: To build a tool that looks up live stock prices, you should use ______ instead of fine-tuning.
exercises.explain: Because prices change constantly, you need RAG to search the live database. Fine-tuning would lock in outdated prices.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI assistants is using RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)?

options.text: An AI that writes Shakespearean poetry.
An AI that answers customer questions by searching through a company policy manual.
An AI that formats raw text into neat JSON data.

exercises.explain: The second option describes a pure retrieval task (looking up external documents), which is RAG. The others require style and behavioral adjustments.

exercises.prompt: You are building a customer-support bot for an airline. It needs to look up real-time flight times AND speak in a very specific, polite brand voice. What is the best strategy?

options.text: Use only RAG because tone does not matter.
Use only Fine-Tuning because facts do not matter.
Use RAG to fetch the latest flight data, and Fine-Tuning to teach the bot the polite customer-service tone.
Neither will work; you must write all answers by hand.

exercises.explain: RAG is best for keeping information current without retraining, while Fine-Tuning is perfect for teaching a specific style, tone, or format. Since you need both real-time data and a specialized tone, a hybrid approach works best!
